mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production indicate expanding manufacturing activity and supply capacity, which typically boosts corporate earnings and equity prices.
failure_condition: Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows remains below 0.03, or directional accuracy below 52% over all instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above the natural rate signals labor market slack and declining aggregate demand, leading to lower corporate profits and negative equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: Mean SPX 60-day return in triggered instances is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or win rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high inflation and low unemployment create a stagflationary threat, reducing real disposable income and forcing the Fed to tighten policy, which contracts equity valuations.
failure_condition: Directional accuracy of the joint signal fails to exceed 55% over any 5-year window, or the interaction term in a logistic regression has p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in housing starts from cyclical lows signals resurgent residential investment and broader economic improvement, leading to positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST above 1500 and 3-month moving average rising
failure_condition: Average 60-day SPX return in triggered periods is not statistically greater than the unconditional average (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, supporting higher equity prices.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns exhibit negative correlation with RSAFS changes (Pearson r < -0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate from the effective zero lower bound signals the start of monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations in the very short term.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25% after being below 0.10% for at least 6 months
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward SPX return in the 10 trading days following trigger is not negative (one-sided p > 0.05) or rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in real GDP and rise in unemployment constitutes a classic recession signal, which strongly predicts negative equity returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 0% and UNRATE increases > 0.5% over 3 months
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a negative mean SPX 60-day return with a t-statistic below -2.0 over any 20-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising industrial production can be interpreted as overheating that exacerbates inflation pressures, prompting monetary tightening and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL level > 280 and INDPRO month-over-month increase > 0.5%
failure_condition: The joint signal's 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant difference from baseline (two-sample t-test p > 0.10) or trading strategy based on signal underperforms buy-and-hold by > 2% annualized.
